title: Investigate this repo for the best way to handle globally-disabled Codex slash commands/prompts/skills. Focus on existing launcher/prompt infrastructure, fallback patterns, and what minimal repo changes would make plain-text invocations like 'k', 'kernel', 'vote', or named skills viable. Return conc...
summary_text: `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public` already has a split architecture for this: prompt definitions + shell launcher fallbacks + tests/CI checks. No files were edited. 1) Current prompt + command surfaces - Kernel: - `.codex/prompts/k.md` (defines `/k`, aliases, and kernel startup behavior) - `.codex/prompts/kernel.md` (defines `/kernel`, manifests/constraints, execution profile) - Vote: - `.codex/prompts/v.md` (defines `/v`) - `.codex/prompts/vote.md` (defines `/vote`) - Enforcement/checking: - `scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` - `scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh` - CI gate references these checks in `.github/workflows/fugue-orchestration-gate.yml` - Launcher/install: - `scripts/local/launchers/kernel` - `scripts/local/launchers/k4` - `scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh` - `scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh` - `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh` - Tests showing current fallback/runtime expectations: - `tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` - `tests/test-kernel-launcher-template.sh` - `tests/test-kernel-entrypoint.sh` - `tests/test-codex-orchestrator-snippet.sh` 2) How fallback currently works - Prompt command path depends on prompt loading (`/k`, `/kernel`, `/vote`) and is validated by script checks. - If launcher flow cannot open existing run context, it falls back through explicit tiers (`open` -> `new` -> guarded launch -> prompt launch command). - `codex-prompt-launch` is already used as fallback for prompt entry in launcher scripts, so plain-text invocations can be routed without relying on slash parsing if they are surfaced via launcher/symlink layer. 3) Skills enable/disable path - Skills are synced/imported through: - `config/skills/fugue-openclaw-baseline.tsv` - `config/skills/googleworkspace-cli-baseline.tsv` - `scripts/skills/sync-openclaw-skills.sh` - `scripts/skills/sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh` - This is the right place to enforce globally-disabled handling for named skills, with controlled skip/allow behavior and logs. Recommended minimal approach (non-invasive + highest reliability) 1. Keep slash prompts as authoritative and fix bootstrap/load path first: 1) Run `scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh` and ensure `.codex/prompts` + launcher artifacts are installed. 2) Validate with existing checks/tests: - `scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` - `scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh` - `tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` 2. Add plain-text front-door mappings for `k`/`kernel`/`v`/`vote` that bypass slash parsing: 1) Extend launcher layer (same pattern already used for kernel entrypoints) so these tokens invoke `codex-prompt-launch <command>` directly. 2) Wire/install through `install-kernel-launchers.sh` so behavior is reproducible. 3) Add/extend one launcher test to assert plain tokens resolve to same prompt path as slash equivalents. 3. Implement disabled-skills behavior in sync/import layer, not launcher layer: 1) In `scripts/skills/sync-openclaw-skills.sh` and `scripts/skills/sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh`, add a small denylist/allowlist policy at import time. 2) If skill is globally disabled: skip with explicit log and continue, do not hard-fail the whole bootstrap. 4. Keep command surface discoverable: 1) Update `README.md` and `CODEX.md` sections for plain-text fallback commands so operators know which tokens are guaranteed without slash mode. Why this is the best route - Uses existing tested invariants (checks + launcher tests) instead of inventing a new command parser. - Preserves prompt semantics while adding an input-format fallback path (`k`, `kernel`, `vote`, `v`) that works even if slash command parsing is disabled globally. - Keeps skill disablement isolated to skill sync, which is the correct domain boundary.
